opp.ais_why:
• On the 12/18/2025 call, champion Karen Pagorek pushed Arthur to define autonomous and agentic terms more precisely, specifically asking him to disentangle e-auctions, tactical spend, and autonomous sourcing with negotiation inside iSource. That terminology pressure is substantive AI dialogue from the buyer side, not surface-level interest.
• Karen then asked what a pilot might look like. Arthur proposed pilots covering iSource, autonomous sourcing, and spend analysis: a sandbox RFX event, an MRO category negotiation, and a 3-month spend cube prototype. Karen also pressure-tested ROI realism on the $48M savings claim, calling for transparency on the assumptions.
• The offsetting signal is that the three-prong AI POC was paused on 5/11/26. That is a real down-signal post-call. The buyer is still at "quantify incremental value" and remembers poor feedback from past supplier evaluations. Active autonomous-sourcing dialogue offsets the pause, but the pause is why this stays in the cautious band.

items.source: SF OpportunityContactRole (Joshua Jepsen) vs call/activity history

metrics.narrative: The value thesis is hard-savings driven and buyer-articulated but not yet modeled against John Deere's own baseline. Eduardo Viera confirmed on 30 Sep 2025 that funding was 'secured' in 2024 and is backed by 'hard savings or hard numbers, incremental cost reduction and organizational optimization.' On the 18 Dec 2025 proposal review Karen Pagorek pressure-tested the headline ROI, calling the 40 percent sourcing-cycle reduction and $48M savings claim 'pretty optimistic' and asking to 'reset based on what our current environment is.' Arthur offered a 70 to 80 data-point value-engineering benchmark (AP cycle time, sourcing time, contract time, compliance) versus Hackett and APQC, but that John-Deere-specific model has not been delivered, so quantified ROI against their baseline is still the open move.

champion.narrative: Engagement is real but no power-champion is selling Zycus internally over the alternatives. Karen Powers (Digital Product Manager, Global Supply Chain, Influencer) was the warm entry and praised the demo, but she enables tools rather than owning budget. Eduardo Viera (Executive Sponsor and primary contact) is the strongest advocate and the path to power, but his support is conditional on make-versus-buy fit and he has not pushed the deal forward internally. Karen Pagorek (Global Strategic Sourcing Manager, Evaluator) became the day-to-day owner but is currently the person who paused the POC. The deal is effectively single-threaded through a rotating evaluator set; developing Eduardo into an active sponsor who brokers the CFO is what is missing.

competition.narrative: This is primarily a build-versus-buy contest with a fresh external entrant, not a classic suite bake-off. The strongest current threat is the unnamed external I/O provider John Deere began deep-diving on 11 May 2026, which directly triggered the POC pause; its identity is unknown and must be uncovered on 20 Jul. The persistent alternative is John Deere's in-house build (SAP-UI engine, iProcure, plus OpenAI/Ultrametrics/Worldlink partnerships) that Eduardo said made 'tremendous progress' in 2025; the wedge is to position Merlin agentic intake and autonomous negotiation as modular accelerants over that build. GEP is the named peer threat in spend analysis (the three-prong plan pilots spend analysis 'to counter GEP'). Oracle and SAP Ariba are logged in Salesforce Competitors__c but absent from every recent call, so they rank low/dormant, and the rep's Coupa reference was a hypothetical, not a buyer preference.

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is well articulated by the buyer. Karen Powers (24 Jun 2025) described a primarily homegrown, heavily customized ('deorized') procurement stack that John Deere now wants to shift toward a mix of purchased solutions to avoid falling behind on AI transformation: 'if you really want to go fast and transform from an AI standpoint, is building everything yourself the right direction? Probably not.' The pain owners are the supply-chain digital-transformation organization (roughly 2,000 users) and the indirect/MRO categories where ad-hoc, un-negotiated spend leaks savings. Eduardo Viera reinforced the financial pain: the investment is justified on incremental hard-savings cost reduction and elimination of manual work. Confirmed operational and financial pain; the gap is quantifying it against their own baseline.

sources: SF activity (SAP S/4HANA integration concern, 18 Jun 2025)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are genuinely unknown because the deal has not reached commercials. The 18 Dec 2025 proposal was explicitly a 'budgetary estimate, not anywhere near a final commercial proposal,' with license and services flexibility still open, so no MSA, SOW, legal, or InfoSec track has started. A relevant forcing function surfaced in activity: an SAP S/4HANA integration concern was raised (18 Jun 2025) and John Deere's SAP IT team is expected to view Zycus 'as competition in a major way' per Eduardo, meaning InfoSec and integration scoping will be contested when they begin. Visibility here requires reaching the 20 Jul reconnect and converting a scoped proof of value into a commercial motion.

economic_buyer.narrative: Two economic-buyer candidates are mapped in Salesforce but neither has committed budget on a call. Eduardo Viera (Global Director, Supply Management Indirect Materials and Services, primary contact and Executive Sponsor) controlled the make-versus-buy and partnership strategy on 30 Sep 2025, framing his stance as 'if there is a solution working already in the marketplace that we don't need to reinvent, let's try to see how we can adapt that,' but his sponsorship is conditional on fit and he has not driven the deal internally. Joshua Jepsen (SVP and Chief Financial Officer, Executive Sponsor) is the ultimate budget authority for the $450k but has never appeared on any call or activity. Directly re-engaging Eduardo to broker access to Jepsen, and confirming who signs, is the key move.

decision_process.narrative: Read from the call sequence, John Deere is running an internal build-versus-buy assessment rather than a formal competitive tender. Eduardo Viera said on 30 Sep 2025 'at this point I don't think we are looking at a competitive... we are very concentrated on what we are doing now,' and proposed a joint on-site workshop (including their SAP technical team) to test where Zycus modules accelerate their roadmap. That workshop path was overtaken on 11 May 2026 when Karen Pagorek paused the POC to re-assess priorities and evaluate another provider, with a 20 Jul 2026 reconnect as the next gate. The process is buyer-controlled and currently in a re-prioritization holding pattern; the approval chain to Jepsen/CFO for a $450k commitment is unmapped.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are clear on functionality but unresolved on proof of value. Across the 24 Jun 2025 demo and 18 Dec 2025 proposal review the buyer prioritized: modular fit that complements their homegrown build rather than replacing it, Merlin agentic intake over Microsoft Teams routing into their existing SAP-UI engine, autonomous sourcing/negotiation on indirect and MRO spend, iSource for strategic RFX, and spend analysis with actionable insight (Karen Powers: 'what I keyed in on was the insights and then what actions that would lead a person to go do'). Karen Pagorek added a hard commercial criterion, that ROI be demonstrated against John Deere's own metrics, not vendor benchmarks. Criteria are confirmed on capability; the decisive criterion is now a credible, scoped proof of value.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. It's genuinely competitive and could go either way; the reads below say where it stands.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Thin signals pull the read to 53.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Champion is driving it — Engagement is real but no single power-champion is driving the deal for Zycus. Eduardo Viera is the strongest advocate (primary Executive Sponsor) but conditional on build-versus-buy fit and not pushing internally; Karen Powers is enthusiastic but lacks budget authority; Karen Pagorek owns the day-to-day but is the one who paused the POC.
bullets.text: Champion is driving it — Engagement is real but no single power-champion is driving the deal for Zycus.

blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.
blocks.title: Deal momentum

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Recency-weighted, the live threat is the unnamed external I/O provider John Deere began deep-diving on 11 May 2026 (it caused the POC pause), alongside John Deere's own in-house build that Eduardo confirmed made 'tremendous progress' in…

bullets.text: Economic buyer unengaged: CFO Joshua Jepsen never on a call; Eduardo Viera's sponsorship is conditional and not internally driven
Unnamed external I/O provider being deep-dived caused the POC pause; identity and positioning unknown
Buyer paused the central POC and is re-prioritizing; close date slipped twice to 22 Feb 2027
SAP S/4HANA integration concern raised and John Deere's own SAP IT team expected to view Zycus as competition

items: Run the 20 Jul reconnect with Karen Pagorek to diagnose the POC halt and name the rival provider. (by 20 Jul)
Deliver the John Deere-specific value-engineering benchmark modeling ROI against their own baseline metrics. (by 10 Jul)
Re-engage Eduardo Viera to broker a power sponsor and a path to CFO Joshua Jepsen. (by 31 Jul)
blocks.title: What moves it forward

contributions.evidence: Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 32 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found; Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 53 is carried by champion strength +0.30 (weight 15); exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: preference strength -0.50 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 55 (moving forward): customer requested next meeting (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 29 is anchored on the stage win (53), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 55, and dragged by risk 32. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: customer requested next meeting (from call evidence).

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 6.0, AIS_Status Strong. Calls corroborate genuine AI appetite (agentic intake over their SAP build, autonomous MRO negotiation, spend insight-to-action), though the buyer wants proof against their own baseline before committing. The paused POC tempers, but does not contradict, the strong AI signal.

business_case.evidence: ROI discussed ($48M savings, 40% sourcing-cycle reduction) but the buyer flagged it as too optimistic and unvalidated against John Deere's own baseline; qualitative hard-savings pain confirmed, numbers not yet modeled.

items.change: Karen Pagorek paused the POC and said John Deere is re-assessing priorities while evaluating another I/O provider
Buyer replied by email (Eduardo Viera): 'We are evaluating options and Zycus is one of those; Karen will share more in your next call' (two-way engagement live)
20 Jul 2026 reconnect with Karen Pagorek accepted to diagnose POC-halt ramifications (re-engage, in-house build, or competitive loss)
Stage moved Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted; forecast moved Pipeline to Best Case

deal_movement.summary: Two-way engagement is live but the central workstream is stalled: the buyer paused the POC on 11 May 2026, the close date slipped twice (to 22 Feb 2027), and the next real touch is the accepted 20 Jul reconnect.

reasons.detail: John Deere paused the POC and is re-assessing priorities while deep-diving a rival external I/O provider, and the $450k Best-Case deal has gone quiet with 29 days of buyer silence logged on 22 Jun. There is no real touch until a 20 Jul reconnect with Karen Pagorek and an Aug 5 workshop meant to restart it. If ignored, this Shortlisted deal drifts to a do-nothing / in-house-build loss with no power champion driving it.
reasons.metric: 29 days buyer silence; $450k at risk; POC paused
reasons.ceo_ask: Ask Arthur why the John Deere POC was paused, whether the 20 Jul Karen Pagorek reconnect is confirmed, and how he restarts momentum before the Aug 5 workshop.
reasons.summary: $450k John Deere stalling - POC paused, 29-day buyer silence
reasons.evidence: Meeting 2026-06-22: '29-day buyer silence (last touch 2025-06-25) ... Workshop Aug 5 is the resta[rt]'; Next_Step: 'Karen Pagorek shared that they are re-assessing priorities and that the POC will not move forward.'

reasons.detail: On 25 Jun the team flagged three overdue Zycus commitments - starting with the business-case value model - due before 02 Jul 2026; these are ours to deliver, not buyer-dependent. With the POC already paused, leaving our own deliverables unmet gives John Deere no reason to re-engage on the 20 Jul call and cedes ground to the rival external I/O provider they are deep-diving.
reasons.ceo_ask: Ask Arthur whether the three overdue Zycus commitments (starting with the business-case value model) were delivered before the 2 Jul deadline.
reasons.summary: Three overdue Zycus commitments to John Deere unmet before 2 Jul deadline
reasons.evidence: Task 2026-06-25: 'Deliver the three overdue Zycus commitments immediately (within next 7 days, before 02 Jul 2026): (1) business case valu[e]...' - deadline now passed with no delivery logged.

critical_signals.text: John Deere began a deep-dive on an unnamed external intake/orchestration provider on 11 May 2026, which is what triggered the POC pause.
On the 11 May call Karen Pagorek paused the proof of concept and said the team is re-assessing priorities, accepting only a 20 July touch base.
The buyer challenged the $48M savings and 40 percent cycle-time claims as too optimistic and wants the case proven against John Deere's own baseline.
CFO Joshua Jepsen holds the budget for the $450k but has never joined a call, leaving the economic buyer unengaged.
The core rival is John Deere's own in-house build, which Eduardo Viera said made tremendous progress in 2025 and only wants Zycus to accelerate.

items.expected_effect: Removes the ROI-credibility objection and gives the buyer a defensible reason to restart the POC.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is two-way and recent (buyer email 12 Jun, accepted 20 Jul connect) but the momentum-critical POC is paused and the last substantive buyer call was 18 Dec 2025, so signals are cooling.

north_star_verdict.math: About 13 months in cycle, Shortlisted since 05 Dec 2025, close 22 Feb 2027 (234 days out), so the date is not the binding constraint. The binding issue is buyer momentum: Karen Pagorek paused the POC on 11 May 2026 and said John Deere is re-assessing priorities while evaluating another I/O provider. A buyer-initiated pause on the one moving workstream, with no accepted commercial proposal and no economic buyer ever on a call, is Slowing.
evidence: SF Next_Step (Karen Pagorek, 11 May 2026): the POC will not move forward, John Deere is re-assessing priorities, and is deep-diving another external I/O provider; a 20 Jul connect is accepted and the outcome could be re-engage.

Buyer incoming email 12 Jun 2026 (Eduardo Viera, via Clari): 'We are evaluating options and Zycus is one of those. Karen will be sharing more details in your next call.' confirms two-way engagement is live, not dark.
Best Case on a deal with a buyer-paused POC, no accepted commercial proposal, and zero economic-buyer meeting attendance is not defensible; the honest category is Pipeline.
north_star_verdict.headline: Live build-vs-buy deal that the buyer paused on 11 May 2026 to deep-dive an unnamed external I/O provider. The relationship is genuinely two-way (buyer email 12 Jun 2026, accepted 20 Jul connect), but the central proof point, the POC, is explicitly stalled and the 20 Jul reconnect is the gate that decides re-engage versus loss.

flags: Multi-thread to Eduardo Viera and secure a power sponsor with a line to CFO Joshua Jepsen; the deal runs through rotating evaluators with no committed advocate.
Uncover the unnamed external I/O provider driving the 11 May POC pause and build a specific counter-wedge before the 20 Jul reconnect.
Restart momentum with one tightly-scoped free proof of value (3-month spend-analysis cube or an MRO autonomous-negotiation event) to counter GEP and prove ROI on John Deere's own data.
best_practice_check.summary: The deal is live but single-threaded and stalled on proof; the highest-leverage levers are to multi-thread to a power sponsor, uncover the new rival, and restart momentum with a scoped free proof of value.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer paused the POC and is deep-diving another external I/O provider: 'they are re-assessing priorities and the POC will not move forward' (Karen Pagorek, 11 May 2026).

competitive_position.summary: Recency-weighted, the live threat is the unnamed external I/O provider John Deere began deep-diving on 11 May 2026 (it caused the POC pause), alongside John Deere's own in-house build that Eduardo confirmed made 'tremendous progress' in 2025. GEP is the dated peer threat in spend analysis. Oracle and SAP Ariba are logged in Salesforce but absent from all recent calls, so they rank low/dormant; Coupa was only a rep hypothetical and has faded.

competitors.name: In-house build / status quo (SAP-UI engine, iProcure, OpenAI/Ultrametrics/Worldlink partnerships)
competitors.quote: 2025 has seen tremendous progress... the team has a clear, very good vision of where we are going, and for me Zycus is that potential partner to accelerate what we don't have in our portfolio.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Merlin intake plus autonomous negotiation as modular accelerants over their SAP build; make continued pure-build's cost and timeline explicit versus faster realized value.

competitors.how_we_win: Lead with AI/agentic depth (32 AI patents) and configurability versus legacy suite rigidity.

competitors.how_we_win: Differentiate on Merlin intake and autonomous negotiation; note John Deere's own SAP IT team will view Zycus as competition, so anchor on complement-not-replace.
